channel.title: Vandal Preachers
channel.description: An actual play D&D podcast following the Vandal Preachers on their adventures.
channel.itunes:author: Vandal Preachers

item.title: Episode 002: Formal Complaint, Re: The Trading Post (Ref. No. TP-12, Calibration Incident)
item.description:
TO: Outpost Administration, Town Hall, Frosthaven FROM: The Undersigned, Senior Trade Clerk, Trading Post (Lv3) DATE: Campaign Week 46, Summer (mud still present; situation unchanged) RE: Incident of this week involving the party member known as "Lava Rock"
I write to formally document the following, as I believe the Town Hall was constructed for precisely this purpose.
On the afternoon in question, the party member identified as Lava Rock entered the Trading Post during business hours — which I will note are posted on the door, in ink, on a board I personally sealed against the damp. He did not browse. He did not purchase. He approached the counter and asked, without preamble, whether I had "heard about the herb situation."
I confirmed that I had read the notice.
He then asked whether I felt the herbs had been "exceptional, or merely competent." I told him I had not personally evaluated the herbs. He asked whether, in my professional estimation, anyone could have pointed the herbs in a direction. I said I did not follow the question. He said he was looking for calibration.
I asked what he was calibrating.
He said: "Recognition. Its distribution. Specifically, whether it's going to the right places."
I told him I was a trade clerk.
He said: "So you understand allocation."
He stood at the counter for a further four minutes. He did not purchase anything. He left when the party member identified as Riptide appeared in the doorway and said, with apparent authority, "Rock. We're going."
Lava Rock went.
I have since re-read the notice three times and remain uncertain what I was supposed to say.
*— Filed in triplicate, per policy*
*[Added below in a different hand, smaller, near the bottom of the page]*
The party member identified as Bamm-Bamm also entered briefly. He looked at the shelves. He nodded once. He left.
*This office has no formal procedure for that.*
